steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures to connect
steps.content: Two independent clauses exist in the sentence: “Sei Shōnagon composed many [word]” and “she bemoans boats run aground, toppled trees, and defeated wrestlers,” with a quotation and relative clause between them.

steps.title: Apply the rule and select the answer
steps.content: Option C (lists. In) correctly separates the two clauses: “Sei Shōnagon composed many lists.” ends the first sentence, and “In “Things That Have Lost Their Power,” which appears in her tenth-century account of Japanese courtly life, Pillow Book, she bemoans...” begins the second. The preposition “In” introduces a prepositional phrase that properly leads to the main clause “she bemoans...” A and D place a semicolon or colon before a quotation (these punctuation marks require an independent clause to follow), and B fails to create a sentence boundary. That matches choice C — C is correct.
